translation: Arjuna said: What is the real structural status of those individuals, O Krishna, who discard the formal technical injunctions of the written laws but continue to perform their worship and sacrifices filled with deep internal faith (shraddhayanvitah)? Does their orientation belong to pure goodness (Sattva), restless passion (Rajas), or dark inertia (Tamas)?
simple_explanation: Arjuna asks about people who act with genuine devotion and faith but do not strictly follow formal scriptural rules or technical codes. He wants to know if their mindset falls under goodness, passion, or ignorance.
life_application: Reflect on your personal practices. Sincerity matters, but it is important to understand whether your unconventional approaches stem from true goodness, restless convenience, or plain laziness.

translation: The Blessed Lord said: The innate, built-in faith (Shraddha) of all embodied human beings is of three distinct types, originating directly from their own psychological modifications and past character conditioning (svabhava-ja). It is classified as Sattviki, Rajasi, and Tamasi. Listen now to My explanation of this system.
simple_explanation: Sri Krishna explains that human faith and deep-seated convictions are not uniform; they are shaped by past conditioning and biology into three types: pure goodness, restless passion, and dark inertia.
life_application: Pay attention to your automatic beliefs and attractions, as they are heavily influenced by your past habits, environment, and physical health.

translation: The internal faith and conviction of every human being, O descendant of Bharata, is shaped in perfect accordance with the configuration of his inner psychological organ (sattvanurupa). A human being is fundamentally built out of his convictions (shraddha-mayo-ayam purusho); whatever his core faith and value-system is, that exactly determines his character and identity.
simple_explanation: A person's character is defined entirely by their deep core values and faith. We are what we believe in; our inner convictions dictate our actions, mindset, and ultimate destiny.
life_application: Audit your core priorities. If you value money above all else, your life becomes transactional; if you value truth and service, your life becomes impactful and fulfilling.

translation: Those who are anchored in Sattva honor and align themselves with the radiant cosmic powers (Devas); those dominated by Rajas worship powerful worldly guardians and ambitious, aggressive titans (yaksha-rakshansi); while those trapped inside Tamas worship dead ghosts and low-grade, chaotic spirits.
simple_explanation: People choose their inspirations based on their inner quality: balanced people look up to pure, radiant ideals; passionate, aggressive people chase raw power and ambition; and deluded people focus on chaotic, lower energies.
life_application: Evaluate your role models. Choose to align yourself with people of high integrity and noble ideals rather than power-hungry icons or toxic, destructive subcultures.

translation: Those proud, arrogant human beings who are driven by the intense force of lust, attachment, and raw vanity, and inflict terrifying, monstrous self-mortifications and tortures upon themselves that are entirely unauthorized by rational laws (ashastra-vihitam);
simple_explanation: Some individuals practice extreme, agonizing self-torture driven by pride, ego, and desire. These severe mortifications are unauthorized by reason or moral laws and stem from toxic vanity.
life_application: Avoid extreme, unscientific diets or self-punishing health routines that compromise your long-term physical wellness out of vanity or guilt.

translation: Cruelly weakening and torturing the collective network of physical organs and biological elements inside their own body, and indirectly violating My conscious ray residing within their inner tabernacle—know these unthinking fools to be of fixed demonic resolve (asura-nishchayan).
simple_explanation: People who violently abuse their own bodies are harming the natural elements and disrespecting the divine spark of life living within them. Krishna classifies these individuals as deluded and demonic.
life_application: Treat your body with basic respect by prioritizing healthy rest, proper nutrition, and steady care, viewing your physical form as a temple for the soul.

translation: Even the food choices (aharas) that every individual prefers are of three distinct types. Similarly, the style of their sacrifices, their self-discipline, and their charities are divided into three. Listen to My systematic breakdown of these differences.
simple_explanation: Our basic lifestyle preferences—including the food we eat, the way we work, our self-discipline, and our generosity—are categorized based on our inner operating qualities.
life_application: Understand that everyday habits like choosing meals or managing your budget are directly tied to your current state of mental clarity and calm.

translation: Foods that systematically augment longevity, inner mental clarity (sattva), physical stamina, robustness, structural health, internal comfort, and cheerful pleasantness—which are naturally savory, healthily unctuous, deeply sustaining, and highly agreeable to the digestive system—are preferred by those of Sattviki disposition.
simple_explanation: People who lean toward clarity and peace love nutritious, fresh, savory, and wholesome foods that boost their lifespan, physical energy, mental health, and digestive comfort.
life_application: Incorporate fresh, clean, and nutritious meals into your daily routine to naturally elevate your mental sharpness and physical wellbeing.

translation: Foods that are excessively bitter, stinging sour, heavily salted, burning hot, aggressively pungent, dry, and causing intense internal inflammation—which directly yield physical distress, psychological grief, and clinical disease (dukh-shok-amaya-pradah)—are eagerly sought after by the passion-driven Rajasi individual.
simple_explanation: Passionate, hyperactive minds crave intense flavors—overly spicy, super salty, highly acidic, burning hot, and bone-dry foods that eventually trigger physical pain, anxiety, and inflammation.
life_application: Cut back on extreme junk foods, excessive caffeine, and overly spicy snacks to help calm a hyperactive, stressed-out mind.

translation: Food that has been cooked hours ago and left to go cold (yatayamam), tasteless and processed, foul-smelling, stale, contaminated table remnants, and unhygienic substances clean from an absolute standpoint—such degraded diet is preferred by the inertia-trapped Tamasi mind.
simple_explanation: Minds stuck in laziness and ignorance prefer stale, cold, over-processed, unhygienic, or rotten foods that lack real nutrients and destroy our basic bodily vitality.
life_application: Avoid relying heavily on highly processed, stale, or poorly prepared convenience foods to guard yourself from chronic lethargy and mental fog.

translation: That structural sacrifice which is executed in strict accordance with statutory laws (vidhi-drishto), by individuals completely free from the lower craving for personal rewards, focusing their minds purely on the conviction that 'performing this work is a mandatory social obligation'—that sacrifice belongs to the mode of pure goodness (Sattvikah).
simple_explanation: Work or duties are considered pure and good when done legally, cleanly, and without an obsessive greed for personal validation, driven simply by a healthy sense of responsibility to the community.
life_application: Approach your primary career or civic tasks with deep pride, doing them well because they serve others, not just for a paycheck or praise.

translation: But that sacrifice which is executed with a calculated eye on a personal reward, or performed purely for public exhibition and ostentatious display, O best of the Bharata dynasty, know that project to belong to the restless mode of Rajas.
simple_explanation: Actions that are highly calculated for personal kickbacks, fame, or designed entirely to show off in public come out of a restless, self-serving, and passionate nature.
life_application: When you lead a charity project or help out at work, keep your focus on the actual cause rather than engineering it for a flashy social media post.

translation: That chaotic undertaking which completely flouts written regulations (vidhi-hinam), where no food is produced or distributed to the community, performed without rational chants, providing no economic remuneration to the workers (adakshinam), and entirely devoid of genuine conviction—that project is designated as Tamasi.
simple_explanation: Projects that completely ignore rules, fail to benefit the group, exploit workers financially, and lack any real faith or care are labeled as ignorant and destructive.
life_application: Ensure any group venture or business project you lead honors rules, respects and pays employees fairly, and provides tangible value to society.

translation: Honoring and serving the cosmic powers, the learned wisdom-bearers (dvija), the mentors (guru), and the enlightened sages; maintaining absolute physical cleanliness, straight integrity, the sacred vow of self-restraint (Brahmacharya), and non-violence—this is designated by the title of austerity of the physical body (shariram tapas).
simple_explanation: Physical discipline means respecting teachers and mentors, maintaining deep cleanliness, living with honesty, practicing healthy self-control, and refusing to hurt other living things.
life_application: Show deep respect to your guides, keep your immediate living spaces perfectly clean, and practice self-control over your physical urges.

translation: Speech that never provokes panic or distress in others (anudvegakaram), which is unyieldingly truthful (satyam), spoken with gentle kindness for the long-term benefit of the listener, accompanied by the regular chanting and study of scientific scripture—this is called the austerity of speech (vangmayam tapas).
simple_explanation: Discipline of speech means telling the truth without weaponizing it to cause fear or pain. Words should be helpful, honest, balanced, and backed by regular reading of verified knowledge.
life_application: When offering feedback or criticism, ensure your words are direct and constructive without turning cruel, avoiding petty gossip or clickbait hysteria.

translation: Serene serenity of mind (manah-prasadhah), gentle kindness, absolute control over vocal expressions (maunam), mastery over lower thoughts, and complete, pristine purification of human motives and emotions—this is designated by the title of austerity of the mind (manasam tapas).
simple_explanation: Mental discipline involves keeping your thoughts calm and peaceful, cultivating kindness, practicing silent focus, controlling passing desires, and cleansing your emotional motives.
life_application: Practice intentional silence or brief digital detoxes to slow down your racing thoughts and keep your inner emotional motives clean.

translation: When this threefold austerity is practiced with intense, sublime faith by integrated human beings who are entirely free from the craving for personal returns, sages designate that discipline as Sattvikam.
simple_explanation: When physical, verbal, and mental self-discipline are practiced with pure focus and deep faith, without looking for public praise or transactional returns, it is pure and good.
life_application: Keep refining your mind, words, and body quietly, finding deep joy in self-improvement rather than hunting for attention.

translation: That self-discipline which is executed purely to capture public adulation, honor, and formal worship, driven by ostentatious vanity, is declared here to belong to the volatile, short-lived, and unstable mode of Rajas.
simple_explanation: Discipline that is performed loudly just to command public applause, unearned respect, or fake prestige is unstable, fleeting, and passion-driven.
life_application: Be honest about your motivations; avoid taking on difficult, showy challenges purely to impress your peers or climb social ladders.

translation: That austerity which is executed out of a foolish, deluded misconception (mudha-grahena), involving the violent torture and mutilation of one's own physical body, or directed toward the destruction and injury of an adversary, is classified as Tamasam.
simple_explanation: Discipline that is based on dangerous misconceptions, causing harm or injury to your own physical health, or aimed at hurting an opponent, is ignorant and dark.
life_application: Never use self-improvement as a weapon to make others feel bad, and avoid harmful, self-destructive patterns of guilt or extreme stress.

translation: That charity which is given with the clean conviction that 'giving support here is a mandatory righteous duty,' distributed to a thoroughly deserving recipient from whom absolutely no commercial return is expected, and allocated at the correct geographical space, correct historical hour, and onto a worthy candidate (patre)—that charity is recorded as Sattvikam.
simple_explanation: Pure giving means sharing your wealth out of duty with a deserving person who cannot pay you back, selecting the right time, location, and a valid cause.
life_application: When supporting a charity or helping a peer, check that the organization is fully transparent, and give unconditionally without expecting favors later.

translation: But that charity which is distributed with a calculated expectation of a future favor in return, or with an eye on a personal reward, or given with a heavy, grudging, and agonizing heart (pariklishtam)—that charity is recorded as Rajasam.
simple_explanation: Giving money or help with transactional expectations, hunting for future business favors, or parting with your wealth with a bitter, defensive, and grudging heart is passionate giving.
life_application: If you choose to donate or support someone, do it happily; if you find yourself feeling bitter or resentful about it, evaluate your true motives.

translation: That charity which is distributed at an inappropriate location, at an improper hour, cast blindly onto highly undeserving, destructive candidates (apatrebhyah), or handed out with cold disrespect and arrogant contempt—that allocation is classified as Tamasam.
simple_explanation: Giving at bad times, in shady locations, handing resources to dangerous or deceptive people, or helping others while throwing insults and showy arrogance at them is dark and ignorant.
life_application: Do not fund scams or unverified projects out of emotional guilt, and never treat people with condescending pity when you help them.

translation: 'OM TAT SAT'—this is recorded as the ancient, threefold sonic designation of the Absolute Brahman. By this primordial matrix, the wisdom-bearers, the Vedas, and the grand social sacrifices were orchestrated in early history.
simple_explanation: The three timeless syllables 'OM TAT SAT' represent the ultimate reality. Sages used this sacred focus mantra to guide, validate, and cleanse human endeavors since early history.
life_application: Keep a high, universal standard in your daily mind, using it to cleanse your lifestyle and remember the bigger picture beyond daily stressors.

translation: Therefore, chanting the syllable OM, all the legal acts of sacrifice, charity, and self-discipline prescribed by written statutes are initiated by the earnest seekers of the Divine.
simple_explanation: By focusing on the universal vibe of 'OM', seekers begin their daily duties, work, and acts of charity with an open, centered, and fully focused mind.
life_application: Start your complex work tasks or community projects with a brief moment of quiet mindfulness to center your attention.

translation: Chanting the syllable TAT, completely dropping all expectations for personal profit, these diverse actions of sacrifice, discipline, and charity are executed by seekers longing for absolute liberation.
simple_explanation: By holding onto the value of 'TAT' (meaning 'That belongs to the Whole'), practitioners drop selfish greed and complete their tasks purely for the wider good.
life_application: Remind yourself, 'This work is for the team and the cause, not just my personal ego,' helping you drop toxic workplace anxieties.

translation: The syllable SAT is systematically applied to indicate objective material reality (sad-bhave) and exceptional moral excellence (sadhu-bhave), O Partha! Similarly, the word SAT is integrated when designating a highly commendable, constructive social duty.
simple_explanation: The term 'SAT' is used to define things that are authentically real, morally excellent, constructive, and aligned with timeless righteousness.
life_application: Strive to make your speech, lifestyle choices, and daily interactions 'SAT'—meaning completely honest, authentic, and constructive.

translation: Unswerving steadfastness inside sacrifice, self-discipline, and charity is also designated by the syllable SAT. And any external action executed for the advancement of that divine purpose is definitively called SAT.
simple_explanation: Staying completely solid in your good discipline, work ethic, and helping others is also called 'SAT'. Any action that pushes forward this good focus earns the same name.
life_application: Be consistent with your ethical habits. True character means sticking to your noble values even when nobody is watching you.

translation: Whatever oblation is poured into a fire, whatever wealth is distributed in charity, whatever self-discipline is performed, or whatever action is executed without genuine internal conviction and alignment—it is branded by the title of ASAT (vain, unreal, non-existent), O Partha! It yields absolutely no credit or utility down here in this world, nor in the life hereafter.
simple_explanation: Any work, charity, or self-discipline done without real care, heart, and sincerity is called 'ASAT'—meaning empty and useless. It brings no true value to your mind now, nor does it help your future soul.
life_application: Do not go through life on autopilot just to tick boxes. Put your full attention, sincere care, and real heart into every task you take on.
